title: clarkia amoena subsp. caurina
intro: Clarkia amoena subsp. caurina, also known as the coastal clarkia or purple needlegrass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the evening primrose family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Clarkia amoena subsp. caurina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
